summary: routeNameError and routeDescriptionError are 13-line copy-paste twins differing only in the max-length constant and the 'name'/'description' segment of the message keys.
failure_scenario: Any change to the validation shape (trim rules, profanity check, error JSON format) must now be made twice and can silently diverge. Simpler form: one private helper textFieldError(value: Option[String], maxLen: Int, keyPrefix: String) building keys as s"routebuilder.$keyPrefix.error.length"/".allowed", with two one-line callers.

summary: A 47-line JS reimplementation of the Scala PolylineEncoder (encode + decimate, same maxPoints=60) exists solely for one caller: the guest branch of RouteBuilder.#handleRouteSaved (RouteBuilder.js:1594).
failure_scenario: Two bit-for-bit-compatible encoder implementations in two languages must be kept in sync (the Scala one already feeds signed-in users' thumbnails via getRoutesForUser); a drift produces subtly wrong guest thumbnails with nothing to catch it. Simpler form: have POST /saveRoute's response include encoded_polyline (RouteServiceImpl already has the streets and PolylineEncoder in hand), store that in the guest localStorage record, and delete polylineEncoder.js from the bundle.

summary: The ownership test re-rolls three 7-line inline FakeRequest(PUT ...) blocks (renameByOther:308, rename:324, renameEmpty:338) even though the file's own putRoute helper (line 94) does exactly that, and the 3-line FakeRequest(DELETE ...) incantation is copy-pasted five times (lines 277, 317, 349, 355 plus the slug test).
failure_scenario: Every request-shape change (headers, CSRF, cookies) must be edited in up to eight places. Simpler form: call the existing putRoute(cookies, routeId, body) for the three renames and add a matching one-line deleteRoute(cookies, routeId) helper for the five deletes.

summary: routeThumbUrl hardcodes the same Mapbox Static Images URL recipe (style id cloov4big002801rc0qw75w5g, path-4+3E8BD9-0.9, auto/400x200@2x?padding=30) that RouteBuilder.#thumbnailUrl (RouteBuilder.js:1003) also hardcodes.
failure_scenario: Changing the thumbnail style, path color, or size requires finding and updating two copies in different languages, or dashboard and RouteBuilder cards silently render mismatched thumbnails. Simpler form: build the URL server-side once (e.g. a thumbnail_url field on RouteWithStats, or a shared helper next to PolylineEncoder) and have both the Twirl template and SavedRoutesPanel consume it.

summary: #clearRoute's optional event parameter and the guard `if (e && e.target && e.target.id === 'delete-route-button')` are dead: its only call site is the delete-route-button click listener (line 145), where the condition is always true.
failure_scenario: A reader must reason about a phantom second caller that never logs ConfirmCancelRoute, and the always-true branch reads as conditional logging. Simpler form: drop the parameter and log 'RouteBuilder_Click=ConfirmCancelRoute' unconditionally in the method (or in the listener).

summary: UndoStack's action records are dead weight: RouteBuilder only ever pushes the constant {type: 'waypoint'} (RouteBuilder.js:828) and #undo discards the popped record without reading it — the class is effectively a counter that syncs the Undo button.
failure_scenario: The docs promise a general action-record protocol that nothing exercises, so a maintainer wastes time looking for inversion logic and per-type handling that doesn't exist. Simpler form: make push()/pop() argument/return-free (a depth counter plus the button sync), or inline a #undoableCount into RouteBuilder; reintroduce records only when a second action type actually appears.

summary: #status.neighborhoodsLoaded and #status.streetsLoaded are boolean mirrors of data presence — each is set in the same statement that assigns #neighborhoodData / #streetData and is never true/false independently of it.
failure_scenario: Two parallel facts must be kept in sync forever (a future code path that sets the data but forgets the flag silently breaks the map-load rendezvous in the 'load' handler and the #addWaypoint guard). Simpler form: delete both flags and test this.#neighborhoodData !== null / this.#streetData !== null at the three read sites, leaving #status with only the genuinely independent mapLoaded/…Rendered/pendingRouteRestored latches.

summary: #chosenIds duplicates information already in #streetsInRoute.features (it is exactly the set of street_edge_ids of the drawn features) and must be co-maintained at three mutation sites (#recompute, #emptyRoute, #drawStreetList).
failure_scenario: Any new code path that touches the drawn features must remember to update the Set too, or feature-state clearing leaks 'chosen' streets that stay invisible on the base layer. Simpler form: derive it where needed — new Set(this.#streetsInRoute.features.map(f => f.properties.street_edge_id)) — in a small #clearChosenStates() helper, since the previous features are still in hand at every clearing site.
